cases.title: Atlantic & Indian Oceans
cases.description: Africa is sandwiched between two major oceans.
steps: West: The Atlantic Ocean separates Africa from the Americas.
East: The Indian Ocean separates Africa from Australasia.
They meet at the Cape of Good Hope in South Africa.

cases.title: Mediterranean & Red Sea
cases.description: The water bodies to the North and North-East.
steps: North: The Mediterranean Sea separates Africa from Europe.
North-East: The Red Sea separates Africa from Asia (Arabia).
These seas have been important for trade for thousands of years.

cases.title: The Suez Canal
cases.description: The most famous man-made waterway in Africa.
steps: Function: It connects the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea.
Importance: It provides the shortest route from Europe to Asia without going around South Africa.
